title: Old Caves Crater

infobox.value: is a [cinder cone](/wiki/Cinder_cone "Cinder cone") located in [Coconino National Forest](/wiki/Coconino_National_Forest "Coconino National Forest"), near [Flagstaff, Arizona](/wiki/Flagstaff,_Arizona "Flagstaff, Arizona"). Its name comes from the numerous small caves on the slopes of the cinder cone. A forest of new growth [ponderosa](/wiki/Ponderosa_pine_(plant_community) "Ponderosa pine (plant community)") pines cover the lower slopes of the cone, while a [pinyon-juniper](/wiki/Pinyon-juniper_woodland "Pinyon-juniper woodland") forest covers the upper slopes. A trail leads to the top of Old Caves Crater, providing views of the [San Francisco Peaks](/wiki/San_Francisco_Peaks "San Francisco Peaks") and the surrounding area. In the 14th century, [Native Americans](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States") built a settlement adjacent to the crater.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Muller, Seth. ["The wondrous portals of Old Caves Crater in Doney Park"](https://azdailysun.com/lifestyles/recreation/the-wondrous-portals-of-old-caves-crater-in-doney-park/article_403ec383-e1cc-50bb-be0a-bcd29a552c3e.html). *Arizona Daily Sun*. Retrieved 2018-06-21.

**Old Caves Crater** is a [cinder cone](/wiki/Cinder_cone "Cinder cone") located in [Coconino National Forest](/wiki/Coconino_National_Forest "Coconino National Forest"), near [Flagstaff, Arizona](/wiki/Flagstaff,_Arizona "Flagstaff, Arizona"). Its name comes from the numerous small caves on the slopes of the cinder cone. A forest of new growth [ponderosa](/wiki/Ponderosa_pine_(plant_community) "Ponderosa pine (plant community)") pines cover the lower slopes of the cone, while a [pinyon-juniper](/wiki/Pinyon-juniper_woodland "Pinyon-juniper woodland") forest covers the upper slopes. A trail leads to the top of Old Caves Crater, providing views of the [San Francisco Peaks](/wiki/San_Francisco_Peaks "San Francisco Peaks") and the surrounding area. In the 14th century, [Native Americans](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States") built a settlement adjacent to the crater.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
